why_keep_or_evict: point_in_time trading reconstruction needs recent detail, but long-term online retention is too expensive
daily bars remain analytically useful longer, but do not need premium hot storage indefinitely
official and high-authority text should stay retrievable, but not necessarily online in full text forever
low-grade repost content is primarily attention evidence and should not accumulate as full-text sludge
structured evidence is the durable reusable truth layer after raw text is compacted
identity and business-reference history are foundational and should remain queryable forever
derived intermediate tables are classic storage traps and should default to rebuildable TTL assets
only final audit and handoff artifacts should survive; search exhaust should not become permanent baggage
labels, feature definitions, and state journals are the semantic spine of reproducibility
shadow journals are expensive but essential for later attribution and dispute resolution
